Abstract: This chapter delves into the transformative journey of Blockchain technology, tracing its evolution from a foundation for cryptocurrency to a fundamental force across various industries. It underlines Blockchain’s remarkable growth, projecting its value to exceed USD 3.1 trillion by 2030, thereby embedding itself as a critical component of global GDP. The chapter explains Blockchain’s diverse applications, from enhancing electrical grid reliability to revolutionizing traditional business methods through its decentralized and secure ledger system. This book chapter further explores the technology’s adoption across sectors such as finance, healthcare, transportation, and education, highlighting its potential to streamline operations, ensure data security, and foster innovation. The chapter identifies key challenges faced by Blockchain, including scalability and regulatory hurdles, yet emphasizes the ongoing research and collaborative efforts aimed at overcoming these obstacles. It predicts a bright future for Blockchain, which will be marked by its integration with emerging technologies like Artificial Intelligence and Internet of Things, which promises to unlock new avenues for solving complex problems. © 2025 selection and editorial matter, Aijaz A. Abstract: Cryptocurrency security is an important things in the digital financial landscape, with decentralized blockchain networks presenting both opportunities and challenges. This paper conducts a comprehensive review of cryptocurrency security, examining key challenges such as double spending attacks, 51 % attacks, wallet and exchange hacks, and privacy concerns. Proposed solutions and their implications are also explored, emphasizing the importance of addressing security vulnerabilities to foster trust, stability, and innovation within the cryptocurrency ecosystem. Through collaborative efforts among stakeholders, this research contributes to a deeper understanding of safeguarding cryptocurrency networks in the digital age. © 2024 IEEE.

Authors: Fu, Y.; Zhou, M.; Zhang, L.
Title: DAM: A Universal Dual Attention Mechanism for Multimodal Timeseries Cryptocurrency Trend Forecasting
Affiliations: Duke Kunshan University, Kunshan, Jiangsu, China; Data Science Research Center and Social Science Division, Duke Kunshan University, Kunshan, Jiangsu, China

Abstract: In the realm of distributed systems, blockchain technology has spurred the development of cryptocurrencies, merging enhanced security and decentralization with significant investment potential. Despite their promise, existing cryptocurrency trend forecasting often oversimplifies sentiment data, failing to fully capture the complex interplay between financial market dynamics and external sentiment influences. This paper introduces a novel Dual Attention Mechanism (DAM) for forecasting cryptocurrency trends using multimodal time-series data. Our approach integrates key cryptocurrency metrics with sentiment data from news and social media, analyzed through CryptoBERT, effectively addressing the inherent volatility and prediction challenges in cryptocurrency markets. By combining elements of distributed systems, natural language processing, and financial forecasting, our method outperforms conventional models, such as LSTM and Transformer, by up to 20% in prediction accuracy. This advancement not only deepens the understanding of distributed systems but also has practical implications for stakeholders in the cryptocurrency and blockchain sectors. © 2024 IEEE.

Abstract: Conventional crowdfunding methods frequently involve intermediaries, lack transparency, encounter regulatory challenges, and are not highly secure. Instead, a blockchain-based agreement can be formed, carried out, and enforced between members without the assistance of a third party. To achieve this, creating smart contracts, with blockchain-compatible executable code, is crucial. Additionally, cryptocurrency can be used, for which crypto wallets can securely store private keys, giving users access, control, and ownership over their digital assets while also encouraging decentralization and interoperability. Crypto wallets protect users' assets against cyber threats and unauthorized access by utilizing technologies like encryption, two-factor authentication, and multi-signature support to provide strong authentication and protection. Using a web3 platform like Thirdweb eliminates the need for an administrator by providing automated tools and infrastructure that streamline the creation, management, and deployment of decentralized applications. This study proposes the use of web3 technology to create a multi-user cross-platform blockchain-based crowdfunding DApp which employs Ethereum, smart contracts via solidity and crypto wallets for decentralized fundraising. © 2024 IEEE.

Title: Examining market volatility arbitrage in cryptocurrencies with the perspective of Beldex coin trading dynamics in India
Affiliations: Faculty of Commerce, Periyar Maniammai Institute of Science & Technology, Thanjavur, TN, India; Department of Mathematics, Periyar Maniammai Institute of Science & Technology, Thanjavur, TN, India
Authors with affiliations: Jayanthi, N., Faculty of Commerce, Periyar Maniammai Institute of Science & Technology, Thanjavur, TN, India; Sampath, Prabhu, Faculty of Commerce, Periyar Maniammai Institute of Science & Technology, Thanjavur, TN, India; Durairaj, Umamaheswari, Faculty of Commerce, Periyar Maniammai Institute of Science & Technology, Thanjavur, TN, India; Harikumaran, M., Department of Mathematics, Periyar Maniammai Institute of Science & Technology, Thanjavur, TN, India
Abstract: Cryptocurrency trading has gained significant adhesion in financial markets, making it essential to understand the factors influencing trading intentions. This study investigates the psychological and knowledge-based determinants of trading intentions towards Beldex coins among crypto traders in India. This study aims to evaluate how risk management, hedonic motivation, investment desire, market knowledge, peer participation, and earning desires impact trading intentions. A survey was conducted with 369 crypto traders in India, and multiple regression analysis was employed to analyze the data. The results indicate that all six factors significantly influence trading intentions, with risk management (β = 0.342, p < 0.001) and earning desires (β = 0.378, p < 0.001) having the strongest impact on Indian Cryptocurrency market arbitrage. The regression model explained 53% of the variance in trading intentions (R2 = 0.53). Cryptocurrency market information is analyzed through the CoinGecko tool that provides charts, market capitalization, and blockchain data; multiple regression analysis is utilized to test the hypothesized relationships. This study reveals that traders' investment decisions in cryptocurrencies are primarily driven by financial motivations, including potential high returns, diversification, and inflation hedging, as well as technological factors of decentralized finance, blockchain technology, and digitalized transactions. © The author(s) 2024.
Author Keywords: Beldex coin; cryptocurrency; financial gains; hedonic motivation; market volatility; risk management; trading

Title: The Role of 5G and Blockchain in Binance's Future Development

Abstract: Background: The advent of 5G and blockchain technologies has signified rapid growth in the digital space. Their unique capabilities have already redefined communication and decentralized networks; however, their possible combined role is the subject of this research article.Objective: This article investigates the implications of 5G technology being used for integration with blockchain and how its disruptive nature would affect Binance's cryptocurrency trading platform.Methodology: A mixed-methods research strategy was followed that draws upon data analytics, in-depth case study investigations and expert consultations. The key point was finding out how unparalleled low latencies and high-throughput capacities from 5G would be a procedures-changer for blockchain operations.Results: The results indicate that the network capabilities of 5G can compensate for some blockchain major difficulties, including scalability and energy consumption. Quicker data transfer speeds up validation processes which leads to faster blockchain transaction times. This combination of advantages makes it possible to use microtransactions and facilitates scaling in terms of volume per transaction while retaining good levels of security and decentralization. Furthermore, with opportunities such as rapid cross-border payments and extensive smart contract capabilities available too - Binance certainly seems well-positioned to lead the charge in leveraging this convergence. But then again, the integration is accompanied by other challenges as well - mainly regulatory and technological challenges of innovation brought along by two cutting-edge technologies.Conclusions: The integration of 5th-generation mobile networks and blockchain has massive potential for cryptocurrency projects such as Binance. This partnership is poised to transform the existing blockchain landscape, ushering in a whole new era of fintech experience with an increased capability for speed and operational efficiency along safety systems. © 2024 FRUCT Oy.

Authors: Preethy Jemima, P.; Cyril, C.
Author full names: Preethy Jemima, P. (58852418200); Cyril, Pretty Diana (57222521426)
Title: Malicious Cyber Attacks on Blockchain Handled Using Machine Learning Algorithm
Source title: Communications in Computer and Information Science
Affiliations: SRM Institute of Science and Technology, Kattankulathur, TN, India
Authors with affiliations: Preethy Jemima, P., SRM Institute of Science and Technology, Kattankulathur, TN, India; Cyril, Pretty Diana, SRM Institute of Science and Technology, Kattankulathur, TN, India
Abstract: Most powerful technology of the trending world is Blockchain. Because of it’s shared, immutable ledger that facilitates the process of recording transactions and tracking assets in a business network. It does not gets limited here, it is widely used in Financial Management and Accounting, Record management in government, Healthcare, Banking, supply chain and so on. In simple terms, Blockchain is a collection of useful information (data). No central authority has the single control on it. Each transaction is documented as a “block” of data as it happens. Every block is interconnected with those that came before and after it.Due to its decentralized nature, after each and every transaction the block information gets appended in the longest chain and is made transparent to everyone enhancing the trust. But Blockchain is suffering from many cyber attacks. Many solutions are made to increase the level of security. Even in Jan 2019 majority attack has happened in Ethereum classic. The most rare occurrence of the event is the 51% attack (majority attack) which take the control of the hashing rate or hashing power of the network, potentially causing a network disruption. Leading to the cause of Mining Monopoly. Most of the time this happens when the attacker blocks all transactions from a miner in their own private network before broadcasting their own version to the network. The other possibilities of this majority attack can break the basis terms like Reverse transaction, Double spending attack. The attacker takes the control of not confirming some transaction, changing the block rewards, stealing the coins and even creating the coins. The majority assault, also known as the 51% attack, aims to split the blockchain in order to invalidate completed transactions and tamper with data integrity. Our approach is to provide security of blockchain transactions by following the POS (proof of stake) and tightening the network strength by tracing out the anomalous behaviour in the transaction.Machine learning model helps us to draw the insights and overcome this by training and testing the entire Ethereum classic logs of transaction and by detection algorithm along with the automatic featured engineering helps to identify in prior the occurrence of this rare event. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Switzerland AG 2024.
Author Keywords: Blockchain; cryptocurrency; cyber attacks; Ethereum; security
Publisher: Springer Science and Business Media Deutschland GmbH

Title: A Timeline and Analysis of Recent Bitcoin Money-Laundering Detection Methods

Abstract: Money laundering poses a significant threat to the integrity of financial systems worldwide and with the development of blockchain technology, new avenues for illegal activities have emerged. The accelerated evolution of blockchain technology has outpaced the progression of corresponding regulatory frameworks, creating a fruitful environment conductive to financial crimes. Current anti-money laundering (AML) techniques often struggle to adapt to the complex and dynamic nature of blockchain transactions, continuously proving the need to reassess both technological and legislative approaches. This paper undertakes a critical analysis of the methods used to detect illegal actions performed on the Bitcoin blockchain. We discuss detection taxonomy, as well as its challenges and opportunities, while also providing a timeline of past and current research. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Switzerland AG 2024.

Author full names: Ayoob, Fiyan Mehfil (59420617200); Jose, Melvina (59420472600); Udhayakumar, Shanmugam (57190392554)
Title: Forensic Analysis and Detection of Illicit Transactions in Bitcoin Network
Affiliations: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Chennai Campus, Vengal, TN, India
Authors with affiliations: Ayoob, Fiyan Mehfil, Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Chennai Campus, Vengal, TN, India; Jose, Melvina, Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Chennai Campus, Vengal, TN, India; Udhayakumar, Shanmugam, Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Chennai Campus, Vengal, TN, India
Abstract: Blockchain technology, celebrated for its decentralized architecture and promise of transparency, has also become a conduit for illicit activities. This paper introduces a novel rule-based methodology for detecting illegal transactions within blockchain networks. By analyzing transaction data through parameters such as block range, transaction hashes, and addresses, the approach identifies suspicious patterns including high-value transactions, unusual fee structures, and links to known illicit addresses. Implemented in Python, the methodology has been rigorously tested and validated, achieving an accuracy of 88% with strong precision, recall, and F1 scores. This research advances the field of cybersecurity, regulatory compliance, and law enforcement by providing a practical and computationally efficient framework for detecting illegal activities in Bitcoin transactions. The rule-based framework, utilizing both threshold-based and heuristic rules, offers valuable tools for financial institutions, law enforcement, and regulators to identify and investigate illicit transactions effectively. The study underscores the need for proactive measures in protecting digital ecosystems and proposes future enhancements, including advanced anomaly detection and realtime analysis, to further improve the framework's efficacy and adaptability. © 2024 IEEE.

Abstract: Even though electronic voting significantly decreased election costs and offered some convenience, it has been criticized for being unreliable when compared to the traditional pen and paper method because it is physically possible for someone with physical access to the system to change the results. A central system is also necessary to oversee the entire process, including electronic voting. Election results and tracking of those results. Given how easy it is to target votes, voters are not entirely safe. It also poses a severe danger to voting rights and transparency. This article offers a solution to the problems associated with traditional elections by utilizing blockchain technology, which has become a fascinating technology for various applications due to its distinct qualities that set it apart from other technologies. The aim of this project is to develop a decentralized electronic voting system that verifies voter identity through an honest and transparent voting process and protects voter privacy during data transfer using blockchain technology. © Grenze Scientific Society, 2024.

Abstract: Blockchain was first only utilized to support cryptocurrencies, but as time passes, more and more industries are embracing this new technology. Several other crypto currencies started to emerge piecemeal from bitcoin. With the use of blockchain technology and smart contracts, Defy Systems increases the power of websites. Blockchain is expected to be used as an efficient method of conducting online transactions as technology matures. One of the diligences to which blockchain technology may be applied is crowdfunding sites. A brand-new and cutting-edge method for funding many kinds of bets is crowdfunding, in which the bets' individual writers can ask for money. With the advent of the internet and social media, new platforms emerged. For charitable organizations and business owners looking to grow their wealth, the internet and social media are crucial. In this study, it is proposed as a cutting-edge hybrid blockchain crowdsourcing platform called CrypFunds to further explore the benefits of merging blockchain and crowdsourcing. Cryptocurrency donations, whether they are made to a political party or an association, are frequently accepted in the Contributions in cryptocurrencies can give organizations access to a new group of implicit philanthropists who would rather give via blockchain than some of the more conventional methods that might enable them to avoid capital gains taxes. © Grenze Scientific Society, 2024.

Abstract: Recently, artificial intelligence (AI) and blockchain have become two of the most trending and disruptive technologies. Blockchain technology can automate payment in cryptocurrency and provide access to a shared ledger of data, transactions, and logs in a decentralized, secure, and trusted manner. In addition, with smart contracts, blockchain has the ability to govern interactions among participants with no intermediary or a trusted third party. AI, on the other hand, offers intelligence and decision-making capabilities to machines similar to humans. This review presents a detailed survey on blockchain and AI basics and features. This paper provides a review of the literature and a brief on the integration of blockchain and AI applications in multiple areas. We also identify some sole cases of blockchain–AI integration in the energy sector with current use cases. Eventually, we discuss research advantages and challenges associated with integrating blockchain with AI in the energy domain. © © 2024 Al Shareef, Seçkiner, Eid and Abumeteir.

Authors: Anandaraj, I.; Chembakassery, D.
Author full names: Anandaraj, Ilavendhan (57201447708); Chembakassery, Duane (59396262000)
Title: Proof of Computational Power: An Innovative Consensus Algorithm for Blockchain Systems
Affiliations: Vellore Institute of Technology, Chennai, Chennai, TN, India
Authors with affiliations: Anandaraj, Ilavendhan, Vellore Institute of Technology, Chennai, Chennai, TN, India; Chembakassery, Duane, Vellore Institute of Technology, Chennai, Chennai, TN, India
Abstract: In the era of blockchain technology, the traditional Proof-of-Work (PoW) consensus mechanism, while robust, has faced mounting criticism for its staggering energy consumption. We delve into the inner workings of blockchain and Bitcoin, elucidating the intricacies of PoW, its environmental inefficiency, and inherent limitations. The resource-intensive nature of PoW not only limits transaction throughput but also creates barriers to entry for smaller participants, contributing to centralization risks within mining pools. Furthermore, extensive computational power required by PoW protocols has led to concerns about the concentration of mining power among a few entities, potentially compromising the decentralized nature of blockchain networks. Subsequently, we delve into various alternative consensus mechanisms, including Proof of Stake (PoS), Delegated Proof of Stake (DPoS), Proof of Burn (PoB), and Proof of Elapsed Time (PoET), highlighting their respective advantages and drawbacks. In light of the energy consumption challenges posed by PoW, we propose an innovative consensus model which is the Proof of Computational Power (PoCP) designed to substantially reduce energy waste while upholding security and decentralization. Our model introduces dynamic computational power assessment and weighted-random validator selection using smart contracts and machine learning techniques, offering a sustainable and environmentally friendly alternative for blockchain networks. This paper also provides scope for future research in conducting comprehensive simulations to evaluate the scalability, security, and performance trade-offs of PoCP against existing consensus mechanisms. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Singapore Pte Ltd. 2024.
Author Keywords: Bitcoin; Blockchain; Consensus mechanisms; Cryptocurrency; Proof of work
Publisher: Springer Science and Business Media Deutschland GmbH

Title: Technology Intelligence: Cryptocurrencies and Emerging Technologies
Affiliations: University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee, Milwaukee, WI, United States; Milli Savunma Üniversitesi, Istanbul, Turkey; Department of Business Administration, Ege Üniversitesi, Izmir, Turkey; Maseeh College of Engineering and Computer Science, Portland, OR, United States

Abstract: This study is centered on the thematic analysis of cryptocurrency, a methodical exploration of various themes, patterns, and debates inherent to the cryptocurrency domain. Employing rigorous methodologies, including data mining and data analysis, this study systematically extracts and categorizes themes from an extensive corpus of cryptocurrency-related literature, encompassing academic papers, articles, and discussions. The aim is to discern nuanced insights into diverse dimensions of the cryptocurrency ecosystem, spanning technological discussions (notably blockchain and smart contracts), market trends, legal considerations, security vulnerabilities, adoption challenges, and the consequential impacts on various industries. The research under consideration illuminates the escalating ubiquity of the term cryptocurrency and scrutinizes the publication patterns of esteemed authors within the field of bibliometric research. Our study incorporates an extensive number of academic studies on cryptocurrency, employing a diverse range of methodologies that leverage distinct features of the datasets to unravel multifaceted interpretations of the subject matter. Through these methodological approaches, we have successfully identified prevailing themes, including theoretical controversies, regional publication patterns, and variations in the utilization of databases and analytical tools. Particularly noteworthy is the discernible upswing in the volume of scholarly works and applications dedicated to the realm of cryptocurrencies. These applications and scholarly outputs serve as indispensable instruments for scientific cartography, informing decision-making processes in fields such as finance, management, and sustainability. It is pertinent to highlight the emergent recognition of the concept of “cryptocurrency thematic analysis,” with thematic analysis itself assuming a pivotal role as a valuable tool for researchers, entrepreneurs, and governmental entities. This analytical framework facilitates a nuanced comprehension of the dynamic characteristics of cryptocurrencies, an enhanced understanding of public sentiment, and the pinpointing of specific areas necessitating further research or intervention. © 2025 The Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers, Inc.
Author Keywords: cryptocurrency; Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA); technology management; topic detection

Title: Evaluating Value at Risk in First-Layer Cryptocurrency Token Investments via Monte Carlo Simulation
Affiliations: Bina Nusantara University, Jakarta, Indonesia; Bina Nusantara University, Jakarta, Indonesia
Authors with affiliations: Jonathan, Jason, Bina Nusantara University, Jakarta, Indonesia; Purnama, Priska Arindya, Bina Nusantara University, Jakarta, Indonesia; Aditya Kristamtomo Putra, R., Bina Nusantara University, Jakarta, Indonesia; Syahchari, Dicky Hida, Bina Nusantara University, Jakarta, Indonesia
Abstract: This study investigates the Value at Risk (VaR) in cryptocurrency asset portfolios using the Monte Carlo Simulation method, a crucial metric for measuring potential financial losses. With the cryptocurrency market's significant volatility, especially peaking in 2021, traditional predictive methods fall short in accurately assessing risk. By analyzing historical market movements and simulating thousands of potential future scenarios, this research focuses on first-layer coins, which are fundamental to the blockchain ecosystem. The findings demonstrate that Monte Carlo Simulation provides reliable VaR estimations, capturing the high volatility and unique characteristics of cryptocurrency markets. This method proves effective in generating significant price predictions and aiding individual decision-making processes. The study concludes that Monte Carlo Simulation is the most effective method for VaR estimation in cryptocurrencies, highlighting its role in enhancing financial literacy and developing robust trading strategies. Our aim is to educate the Indonesian and global community on these strategies, opening new opportunities and improving financial security during the global transition to blockchain technology. © 2024 IEEE.
Author Keywords: Cryptocurrency; Monte Carlo; Trading; Value at Risk

Title: Exploring the Mechanisms of Decentralized Finance (DeFi) Using Blockchain Technology
Affiliations: School of Economics and Business, Telkom University, Bandung, West Java, Indonesia
Authors with affiliations: Alamsyah, Andry, School of Economics and Business, Telkom University, Bandung, West Java, Indonesia; Salsabila, Nanda, School of Economics and Business, Telkom University, Bandung, West Java, Indonesia
Abstract: Decentralized Finance (DeFi) has revolutionized the financial landscape through blockchain technology, offering innovative solutions for efficiency and transparency in finance. However, its mechanisms still need to be explored. This study examines DeFi products, services, benefits, and emerging threats through a detailed literature review, phenomenological research, and interviews, presenting valuable insights and proposed solutions. The study aims to be a critical scholarly reference and inspire decentralized innovation by robustly understanding DeFi's evolutionary dynamics. It highlights significant benefits like increased financial inclusion, transparency, and efficiency while addressing risks such as smart contract vulnerabilities, over-collateralization, liquidity challenges, and regulatory uncertainties. The novelty lies in its comprehensive examination of DeFi mechanisms, offering unique insights into user experiences and practical applications. Detailed insights are provided into various DeFi mechanisms, including lending and borrowing, Automated Market Makers (AMMs), yield farming, staking, decentralized exchanges (DEXs), stablecoins, payments, and insurance. Future research should focus on enhancing security measures, exploring scalable solutions, and assessing DeFi's long-term impact on traditional financial systems. By addressing these concerns, DeFi can continue to evolve and provide innovative financial solutions. © 2024 IEEE.
Author Keywords: Blockchain Technology; Cryptocurrency; Decentralized Finance (DeFi); DeFi mechanisms; Financial Industry

Affiliations: Department of Ee and Cs, Howard University, Washington, D.C., United States; Department of CS and DS, Meharry Medical College, Nashville, TN, United States

Abstract: The Fintech industry represents the convergence of finance and technology through innovative digital solutions from mobile banking to cryptocurrency. However, it faces big challenges with keeping data safe and systems strong. Traditional methods struggle to keep pace with sophisticated threats and complexities inherent in modern Fintech ecosystems. This paper proposes an approach to address these challenges using Generative AI and blockchain integration to make Fintech systems more resilient. Advanced machine learning algorithms detect and prevent data tampering in the proposed systems. Generative AI is used for threat detection, anomaly recognition, and real-time monitoring in system security. Then, we integrate blockchain technology to enhance the overall resilience of systems. Blockchain technology enhances the reliability of financial services in secure transactions, validating blocks, and distributing control across decentralized networks. These combined methodologies address the critical challenges of data integrity, security, and system resilience in dynamic Fintech systems. The performance analysis demonstrates the efficacy of our proposed framework in enhancing data integrity, security measures, and system resilience within Fintech systems. © 2024 IEEE.

Authors with affiliations: Murano, Angelo, Università degli Studi di Salerno, Salerno, SA, Italy; Bruno, Bruna, Università degli Studi di Salerno, Salerno, SA, Italy; Vespri, Vincenzo, Università degli Studi di Firenze, Florence, FI, Italy
Abstract: This study aims to explore blockchain technologies by integrating the economic perspective and the computer science approach, with a special focus to economic applications where non-cryptocurrency blockchain are usually adopted. This interdisciplinary approach may help to create blockchain solutions that are both technically robust and economically sustainable. For non-cryptocurrency permissioned blockchains to be widely adopted in various business and organizational applications, it is important to view nodes as economic agents rather than abstract entities. These agents exhibit behaviour driven by their individual interests. Therefore, the consensus protocol must be designed to consider effective incentive compatibility constraints. The theoretical approach is based on the evaluation of incentive compatibility constraints for both honest and faulty nodes, according two different remuneration schemes. By extending the principles of incentive compatibility from non cryptocurrency blockchains to organizational structures modelled as DAOs, we explore how decentralized governance models can benefit from blockchain-based incentive mechanisms while addressing the inherent economic risks and legal challenges. © 2024 Copyright for this paper by its authors.

Title: BITPAYS: Leveraging Bitcoin for Digital Payments to Fuel Economic Growth in Indonesia's Society 5.0 Era
Affiliations: Department of Accounting, Universitas Negeri Jakarta, Jakarta, Jakarta, Indonesia
Authors with affiliations: Khairunnisa, Hera, Department of Accounting, Universitas Negeri Jakarta, Jakarta, Jakarta, Indonesia; Zairin, Gentiga Muhammad, Department of Accounting, Universitas Negeri Jakarta, Jakarta, Jakarta, Indonesia; Budianto, Prabowo, Department of Accounting, Universitas Negeri Jakarta, Jakarta, Jakarta, Indonesia; Ramadhani, Nazila Alvi, Department of Accounting, Universitas Negeri Jakarta, Jakarta, Jakarta, Indonesia; Septiani, Mita, Department of Accounting, Universitas Negeri Jakarta, Jakarta, Jakarta, Indonesia; Musyaffi, Ayatulloh Michael, Department of Accounting, Universitas Negeri Jakarta, Jakarta, Jakarta, Indonesia
Abstract: The swift evolution of information technology in the last two decades has heralded the era of Society 5.0, wherein cryptocurrencies have emerged as forefront innovations. Cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin, as digital assets and payment mechanisms, champion decentralized finance and worldwide connectivity via blockchain technology. The burgeoning interest in cryptocurrencies within Indonesia, underscored by Bappebti's data revealing a positive trajectory with over 17.91 million crypto investors by September 2023, underscores the imperative for innovative applications of Bitcoin in digital payments. This necessity gave rise to BITPAYS, an application devised to bolster the digital economy's growth within the Society 5.0 paradigm. Designed for ease of use, BITPAYS simplifies employing Bitcoin in everyday transactions. With the soaring popularity of cryptocurrencies, BITPAYS aspires to fulfill the demand for pioneering payment solutions. Observing the successful incorporation of cryptocurrencies as payment methods in the United States and Russia, Indonesia stands to draw inspiration, contingent upon the establishment of conducive regulatory frameworks. This research delves into leveraging Bitcoin as a digital payment instrument to foster the digital economy's expansion in the Society 5.0 era. It further explores BITPAYS' application implementation to streamline daily Bitcoin transaction activities. This study aims to underscore Bitcoin's role as a digital payment medium and elucidate BITPAYS application operations. Employing a descriptive analytical methodology and the ADDIE model for BITPAYS application development, this investigation seeks to offer comprehensive insights into cryptocurrency utilization as a digital payment alternative in Indonesia. BITPAYS is anticipated to significantly enhance cryptocurrency investment value and simplify everyday transactions, thereby elevating public consciousness of Bitcoin's utility and advantages as a payment method. © 2024 IEEE.
Author Keywords: Bitcoin; BITPAYS; Cryptocurrency; Cryptocurrency investment; Digital Assets; Digital payment tool
Publisher: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers Inc.

Authors: Silva, A.D.; Thakur, S.; Breslin, J.
Author full names: Silva, Anupa De (59377556100); Thakur, Subhasis (25825693800); Breslin, John G. (7004753954)
Title: An Integrated Stablecoin and Subsidised Node Design for Payment Channel Networks
Affiliations: Data Science Institute, University of Galway, Galway, Connacht, Ireland
Authors with affiliations: Silva, Anupa De, Data Science Institute, University of Galway, Galway, Connacht, Ireland; Thakur, Subhasis, Data Science Institute, University of Galway, Galway, Connacht, Ireland; Breslin, John G., Data Science Institute, University of Galway, Galway, Connacht, Ireland
Abstract: The financial landscape has undergone a profound shift due to the decentralisation and digitisation brought about by cryptocurrencies. As people begin to reevaluate traditional notions of monetary ownership, cryptocurrencies will be poised to play an even more critical role in the future. However, the widespread adoption of cryptocurrencies has been hampered by several challenges, including volatility and scalability. The concepts of Stablecoin and Payment Channel Network (PCN) are promising proposals for these problems, providing effective individual solutions. While PCN addresses the scalability issue of cryptocurrencies, its transaction reliability is not always up to par for widespread use. Similarly, the increasing centralisation of Stablecoins contradicts the fundamental principle of introducing cryptocurrencies. This paper introduces a decentralised model that integrates both approaches to establish a monetary system while mitigating their inherent drawbacks. It encompasses a subsidised node and stablecoin designs for PCN with a dedicated and incentivised layer of entities to facilitate reliable PCN transactions and contribute to stabilising the underlying currency of PCN. It introduces an integrated network-backed stablecoin for PCN, achieving both stability and scalability concurrently. Also, approximately half of the initial funding required to establish payment channels is derived from external sources, ensuring a guaranteed return on investment within an under-collateralised and decentralised environment. Our system has been substantiated through game theory and simulations, demonstrating its capacity to stabilise the coin's value and effectively fulfil the expected functionalities of a currency. Empirical tests have confirmed its retention of PCN's scalability, even with additional steps integrated to implement the proposed protocol. We also highlight its resilience to potential attacks. Our solution will improve transaction reliability, collateralisation, and underlying coin stability, making cryptocurrencies more suitable for widespread adoption. © 2013 IEEE.
Author Keywords: Blockchain; cryptocurrency; game theory; payment channel network; reliability; scalability; stablecoin
Publisher: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers Inc.

Authors: Brauneis, A.; Mestel, R.; Rauch, M.
Author full names: Brauneis, Alexander (55053577000); Mestel, Roland (12766308200); Rauch, Martin (59364461800)
Title: Financial economics of automated market makers
Affiliations: Nottingham Business School, Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, United Kingdom; Universität Graz, Graz, Styria, Austria; Department of Finance, Universität Graz, Graz, Styria, Austria
Authors with affiliations: Brauneis, Alexander, Nottingham Business School, Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, United Kingdom; Mestel, Roland, Universität Graz, Graz, Styria, Austria; Rauch, Martin, Department of Finance, Universität Graz, Graz, Styria, Austria
Abstract: With the inception of Bitcoin in 2009 as the world’s first cryptocurrency, a new era of trustless, censorship resistant and decentralized transfer of assets has commenced. Smart contract capable blockchains like Ethereum allow for the creation of computer code which is stored on the blockchain. The code executes tasks in a transparent and immutable way, without central authorities or third party middlemen. Automated market maker (AMM) liquidity pools are a groundbreaking application built on top of smart contract blockchains, first introduced in late 2018. AMMs facilitate the non-custodial exchange of arbitrary assets that can be represented on a blockchain, or tokenized. This chapter sheds light on the operating principles of AMMs and highlights variants of AMMs and notions of liquidity provision, slippage, and impermanent loss. © The Editors and Contributors Severally 2024.
Author Keywords: Automated market maker; Cryptocurrencies; Decentralized finance; Impermanent loss

Authors: Arjun Chetry, S.; Sharma, U.
Author full names: Arjun Chetry, Sh (59362819400); Sharma, Uzzal (57908425100)
Title: Blockchain Technology, DApps, and the Dark Web: LEAs Investigation Perspectives
Source title: Lecture Notes in Networks and Systems
Affiliations: Assam Don Bosco University, Guwahati, AS, India; Birangana Sati Sadhani Rajyik Vishwavidyalaya, Golaghat, India
Authors with affiliations: Arjun Chetry, Sh, Assam Don Bosco University, Guwahati, AS, India; Sharma, Uzzal, Assam Don Bosco University, Guwahati, AS, India, Birangana Sati Sadhani Rajyik Vishwavidyalaya, Golaghat, India
Abstract: Dark web platform, a portion of deep web, with utmost privacy provided by Tor network poses many challenges to Law enforcement agencies during investigation. As it is part of deep web, only authorized person or person with required credentials are authorized to access the content in the server. However, accessing this dark web platform requires a specialized tools, like tor browsers, tails os, whonix os, etc. This paper explores the roles of different types of dark web markets, decentralized applications and the blockchain technology. With decentralized nature of this platform, it is difficult for investigator to collect the information from intermediary during investigation. Moreover, with cryptocurrencies, the payment node is also anonymous. This paper presents various types of challenges imposed by cryptocurrencies as well as the roles of different cryptocurrencies in dark web markets. Further, different DApps involved in dark web platforms are explored along with their features. The paper concludes with the recommendations for LEAs to understand this dark web platform from the perspective of investigator and also to incorporate few changes in the investigation procedures including collaboration with international agencies, training for investigators and creating public awareness. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Singapore Pte Ltd. 2024.
Author Keywords: Anonymous Communication; Cryptocurrency; Dark Web; Dark web markets; Decentralized Applications; Investigation; Law Enforcement Agencies
Publisher: Springer Science and Business Media Deutschland GmbH

Authors: Akcin, O.; Streit, R.P.; Oommen, B.; Vishwanath, S.; Chinchali, S.
Author full names: Akcin, Oguzhan (57937651000); Streit, Robert P. (57223885136); Oommen, Benjamin (57953418800); Vishwanath, Sriram (35584593600); Chinchali, Sandeep P. (36460799600)
Title: ControlPay: An Adaptive Payment Controller for Blockchain Economies
Affiliations: The University of Texas at Austin, Austin, TX, United States
Authors with affiliations: Akcin, Oguzhan, The University of Texas at Austin, Austin, TX, United States; Streit, Robert P., The University of Texas at Austin, Austin, TX, United States; Oommen, Benjamin, The University of Texas at Austin, Austin, TX, United States; Vishwanath, Sriram, The University of Texas at Austin, Austin, TX, United States; Chinchali, Sandeep P., The University of Texas at Austin, Austin, TX, United States
Abstract: Recent advancements in blockchain technology have led to the development of various decentralized service platforms for various tasks, like machine learning and wireless networks for example. Central to the operation of these platforms is a token-based economy, rewarding service providers with cryptocurrency tokens for their contributions to the setup, verification, and maintenance of a platform. However, these platforms often rely on predetermined token supply strategies which render a platform's operation susceptible to market fluctuations. A more flexible approach, one allowing for dynamic response to changes in system demand and market conditions, is essential to mitigate such vulnerabilities. To address these challenges, we introduce a control-theoretic approach to stabilizing a decentralized service platform's token economy. Specifically, we first model these blockchain economies as dynamical systems where token circulation, pricing, and consumer demand evolve based on payments to service providers and service costs. Then, we utilize our model to introduce ControlPay: a novel payment controller based on model predictive control (MPC) designed to enhance the performance of decentralized networks while simultaneously ensuring token price stability. Additionally, we also examine the impact of strategic behavior in the market through a Stackelberg game to further enhance the robustness of our payment controller. Finally, we evaluate our methodology on real and synthetic data. Our findings show that ControlPay significantly outperforms conventional algorithmic stablecoin approaches, yielding improvements of up to 2.4× in simulations based on actual demand data from existing blockchain-driven decentralized wireless networks. © 2024 IEEE.
Publisher: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers Inc.

Authors: Sharma, T.; Kaur, G.K.; Mahto, H.K.; Kumar, G.; Kumar, A.; Singla, S.
Author full names: Sharma, Tanmay (58978512000); Kaur, Geet Kiran (58893918500); Mahto, Hritik Kumar (59353739400); Kumar, Gaurav (59976167400); Kumar, Abhinanadan (59353313000); Singla, Sanjay (57192179109)
Title: Exploring ApexWolf: A Study of a Cryptocurrency Exchange Website
Affiliations: Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Chandigarh University, Mohali, PB, India
Authors with affiliations: Sharma, Tanmay, Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Chandigarh University, Mohali, PB, India; Kaur, Geet Kiran, Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Chandigarh University, Mohali, PB, India; Mahto, Hritik Kumar, Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Chandigarh University, Mohali, PB, India; Kumar, Gaurav, Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Chandigarh University, Mohali, PB, India; Kumar, Abhinanadan, Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Chandigarh University, Mohali, PB, India; Singla, Sanjay, Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Chandigarh University, Mohali, PB, India
Abstract: The DeFi (decentralized finance) platforms that are coming up present a ground breaking chance in the crypto world. Here the analysis is based on ApexWolf, an Ethereum-only crypto exchange, and its collaboration with DeFi protocols. The system's evaluation uses a methodology that combines user feedback and transaction simulation to assess the security measures, features, and usability. Results are the proof of fast transaction execution, competitive costs, and the positive feedback from the users. The ApexWolf project despite the intrinsic blockchain limitations demonstrates great potential in supporting users to interact in a decentralized finance system. The study provides knowledge about the transformation process of cryptocurrency exchanges and DeFi platforms presenting both opportunities and difficulties for future development and widespread use. © 2024 IEEE.
Author Keywords: blockchain; Cryptocurrency exchanges; decentralized finance (DeFi); Ethereum; security; transaction simulation; UX
Publisher: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers Inc.

Authors: Kim, H.J.; Lee, G.M.; Lee, J.; Kang, S.; Chae, S.W.; Park, J.-S.
Author full names: Kim, Hyoung Joong (57089583800); Lee, Gyu M. (56204679300); Lee, Jongwon (36614531500); Kang, Sora (7405687296); Chae, Seong Wook (36719803300); Park, Junseok (57145151300)
Title: A Comparison of Impermant Loss for Various CFMMs
Affiliations: Department of Digital Finance Management, Hoseo University, Asan, Chungcheongnam-do, South Korea; Department of Industrial Engineering, Pusan National University, Busan, South Korea; Graduate School of Management of Technology, Hoseo University, Asan, Chungcheongnam-do, South Korea; Electronics and Information System Engineering Major, Kookmin University, Seoul, South Korea
Authors with affiliations: Kim, Hyoung Joong, Department of Digital Finance Management, Hoseo University, Asan, Chungcheongnam-do, South Korea; Lee, Gyu M., Department of Industrial Engineering, Pusan National University, Busan, South Korea; Lee, Jongwon, Graduate School of Management of Technology, Hoseo University, Asan, Chungcheongnam-do, South Korea; Kang, Sora, Department of Digital Finance Management, Hoseo University, Asan, Chungcheongnam-do, South Korea; Chae, Seong Wook, Department of Digital Finance Management, Hoseo University, Asan, Chungcheongnam-do, South Korea; Park, Junseok, Electronics and Information System Engineering Major, Kookmin University, Seoul, South Korea
Abstract: Decentralized exchanges are one of the revolutionary inventions in cryptocurrency trading. Decentralized exchanges present a way to set prices mathematically without maintaining an order book. Order books have been a widely-known method of determining prices for over 400 years in the stock markets and even in decentralized cryptocurrency exchanges. Recently, a new type of decentralized exchange devised a constant function market maker to mathematically determine prices. Among them, constant product market makers are the most widely used. In addition, several constant function market makers have been proposed. However, there was no discussion about the desirable requirements of a constant function market maker in terms of impermanent loss. In this paper, we discuss the desirable requirements of a constant function market maker and analyze the impermanent loss of various market makers. Note that two market makers, LMSR and CCMM, show impermanent gain in the mathematical derivation, but their price functions violate the principle of supply and demand. Therefore, AMMs with impermanent gain while following the principle of supply and demand need to be studied further. A comparison study of impermanent loss for some CFMMs shows interesting results, too. © 2024 IEEE.
Author Keywords: blockchain; cryptocurrency; decentralized exchange; impermanent loss; market maker; permanent loss; smart contract
Publisher: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers Inc.

Authors: Burgess, A.; Hamilton, R.; Leuprecht, C.
Author full names: Burgess, Ariel (59346182000); Hamilton, Rhianna (57893665500); Leuprecht, Christian (6506005656)
Title: Terror on the Blockchain: The Emergent Crypto-Crime-Terror Nexus
Affiliations: Queen’s University, Kingston, ON, Canada; Royal Military College of Canada, Kingston, ON, Canada; The University of Adelaide, Adelaide, SA, Australia
Authors with affiliations: Burgess, Ariel, Royal Military College of Canada, Kingston, ON, Canada; Hamilton, Rhianna, The University of Adelaide, Adelaide, SA, Australia; Leuprecht, Christian, Queen’s University, Kingston, ON, Canada, Royal Military College of Canada, Kingston, ON, Canada
Abstract: Inadequate oversight and an inchoate appreciation are giving terrorist groups ready access to transboundary financial transfers by means of virtual currency. This chapter counters the prevailing approach that treats cryptocurrency-enabled crimes, such as terrorism, as monolithic. This chapter demonstrates that terrorist groups are using cryptocurrency and decentralized finance to fundraise and transfer funds in conjunction with the traditional financial system. Since actual case studies are few and data limited, this chapter is a proof of concept: it compares terrorist financing schemes by the Al-Qassam Brigades and Al Qaeda that used virtual assets. The comparison of virtual assets being used finds that standards developed and recommended by the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) are wholly inadequate to contain the proliferation of decentralized finance technology and centralized virtual assets as drivers of the global Illicit International Political Economy (IIPE). FATF recommendations are not sufficiently nuanced, nor are they effective at detecting, disrupting and deterring he nexus of crypto, crime and terror. To make matters worse, FATF members are falling short on implementing even FATF’s inadequate standards. The chapter concludes that FATF needs to: clarify inclusion criteria under the current definition of virtual assets; broaden regulations, improve interagency collaboration, and formulate more nuanced recommendations that are sensitive to crypto-enabled crimes across different criminal activities and criminogenic factors. © The Author(s) 2024.
Author Keywords: Cryptocurrency; Decentralized finance; Illicit International Political Economy; Terrorist networks; Virtual assets
Publisher: Springer Science and Business Media B.V.

Authors: Kiayias, A.; Koutsoupias, E.; Marmolejo-Cossío, F.; Stouka, A.-P.
Author full names: Kiayias, Aggelos (6603298818); Koutsoupias, Elias (6603781914); Marmolejo-Cossío, Francisco J. Javier (57200641407); Stouka, Aikaterini Panagiota (57191224432)
Title: Balancing Participation and Decentralization in Proof-of-Stake Cryptocurrencies
Affiliations: The University of Edinburgh, Edinburgh, Scotland, United Kingdom; University of Oxford, Oxford, Oxfordshire, United Kingdom; Harvard University, Cambridge, MA, United States; Nethermind, London, United Kingdom; Input Output Global, Inc., Longmont, CO, United States
Authors with affiliations: Kiayias, Aggelos, The University of Edinburgh, Edinburgh, Scotland, United Kingdom, Input Output Global, Inc., Longmont, CO, United States; Koutsoupias, Elias, University of Oxford, Oxford, Oxfordshire, United Kingdom, Input Output Global, Inc., Longmont, CO, United States; Marmolejo-Cossío, Francisco J. Javier, Harvard University, Cambridge, MA, United States, Input Output Global, Inc., Longmont, CO, United States; Stouka, Aikaterini Panagiota, Nethermind, London, United Kingdom
Abstract: Proof-of-stake blockchain protocols have emerged as a compelling paradigm for organizing distributed ledger systems. In proof-of-stake (PoS), a subset of stakeholders participate in validating a growing ledger of transactions. For the safety and liveness of the underlying system, it is desirable for the set of validators to include multiple independent entities as well as represent a non-negligible percentage of the total stake issued. In this paper, we study a secondary form of participation in the transaction validation process, which takes the form of stake delegation, whereby an agent delegates their stake to an active validator who acts as a stake pool operator. We study payment schemes that reward agents as a function of their collective actions regarding stake pool operation and delegation. Such payment schemes serve as a mechanism to incentivize participation in the validation process while maintaining decentralization. We observe natural trade-offs between these objectives and the total expenditure required to run the relevant payment schemes. Ultimately, we provide a family of payment schemes which can strike different balances between these competing objectives at equilibrium in a Bayesian game theoretic framework. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Switzerland AG 2024.
Author Keywords: cryptocurrencies; decentralization; delegation games; proof of stake
Publisher: Springer Science and Business Media Deutschland GmbH

Authors: Reno, S.; Turna, M.K.
Author full names: Reno, Saha (57223032106); Turna, Marzia Khan (58308927500)
Title: Utilizing Ethereum-Based Lending System to Prevent Defaulting on Loans
Affiliations: Ahsanullah University of Science and Technology, Dhaka, Bangladesh; Bangladesh Army International University of Science and Technology, Cumilla, Bangladesh
Authors with affiliations: Reno, Saha, Ahsanullah University of Science and Technology, Dhaka, Bangladesh; Turna, Marzia Khan, Bangladesh Army International University of Science and Technology, Cumilla, Bangladesh
Abstract: Borrowers and other interested parties in the loan process can create a number of untrustworthy situations that make traditional lending systems less safe and reliable. Although smart contracts and the decentralized nature of the blockchain make it impossible for predatory lending to occur, the decline in cryptocurrency value may make it possible to pay back loans using less amount of fiat currency than was borrowed. In this research, we develop a blockchain and smart contract-based lending system, including ERC-20 tokens as collateral to mitigate borrower risk. Using the smart contract feature, the mechanism operates autonomously, with no human oversight required. By recording transactions in chronological order, the system fosters trust and openness among its users. The system tracks the currency exchange rate’s volatility and periodically raises the value of ERC-20 tokens to combat the aforementioned issues. At the paper’s conclusion, we take a look at the method’s effectiveness in a handful of test cases and data-specific charts. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Singapore Pte Ltd. 2024.
Author Keywords: Blockchain; ERC-20 tokens; Ethereum; Loan default; Smart contract
Publisher: Springer Science and Business Media Deutschland GmbH

Authors: Mazor, O.; Rottenstreich, O.
Author full names: Mazor, Ori (58918662000); Rottenstreich, Ori (24328397000)
Title: Understanding the Blockchain Interoperability Graph Based on Cryptocurrency Price Correlation
Affiliations: Technion - Israel Institute of Technology, Haifa, Israel
Authors with affiliations: Mazor, Ori, Technion - Israel Institute of Technology, Haifa, Israel; Rottenstreich, Ori, Technion - Israel Institute of Technology, Haifa, Israel
Abstract: Cryptocurrencies have gained high popularity in recent years, with over 9000 of them, including major ones such as Bitcoin and Ether. Each cryptocurrency is implemented on one block chain or over several such networks. Recently, various technologies known as blockchain interoperability have been developed to connect these different blockchains and create an interconnected blockchain ecosystem. This paper aims to provide insights on the blockchain ecosystem and the connection between blockchains that we refer to as the interoperability graph. Our approach is based on the analysis of the correlation between cryptocurrencies implemented over the different blockchains. We examine over 4800 cryptocurrencies implemented on 76 blockchains and their daily prices over a year. This experimental study has potential implications for decentralized finance (DeFi), including portfolio investment strategies and risk management. © 2024 IEEE.
Publisher: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers Inc.

Authors: Guo, Y.; Karthikeyan, H.; Polychroniadou, A.; Huussin, C.
Author full names: Guo, Yue (59329207000); Karthikeyan, Harish (57210885610); Polychroniadou, Antigoni (55332641800); Huussin, Chaddy (59328935900)
Title: PriDe CT: Towards Public Consensus, Private Transactions, and Forward Secrecy in Decentralized Payments
Source title: Proceedings - IEEE Symposium on Security and Privacy
Affiliations: JPMorgan Chase & Co., New York, NY, United States
Authors with affiliations: Guo, Yue, JPMorgan Chase & Co., New York, NY, United States; Karthikeyan, Harish, JPMorgan Chase & Co., New York, NY, United States; Polychroniadou, Antigoni, JPMorgan Chase & Co., New York, NY, United States; Huussin, Chaddy, JPMorgan Chase & Co., New York, NY, United States
Abstract: Anonymous Zether, proposed by Bünz et al. (FC, 2020) and subsequently improved by Diamond (IEEE S&P, 2021) is an account-based confidential payment mechanism that works by using a smart contract to achieve privacy (i.e. identity of receivers to transactions and payloads are hidden). In this work, we look at simplifying the existing protocol while also achieving batching of transactions for multiple receivers, while ensuring consensus and forward secrecy. To the best of our knowledge, this work is the first to formally study the notion of forward secrecy in the setting of blockchain, borrowing a very popular and useful idea from the world of secure messaging. Specifically, we introduce:•FUL-Zether, a forward-secure version of Zether (Bünz et al. , FC, 2020),•PRIvate DEcentralized Confidential Transactions (PriDe CT), a much-simplified version of Anonymous Zether that achieves competitive performance and enables batching of transactions for multiple receivers.•PRIvate DEcentralized Forward-secure Until Last update Confidential Transactions (PriDeFUL CT), a forward-secure version of PriDe CT.We also present an open-source, Ethereum-based implementation of our system. PriDe CT uses linear homomor-phic encryption as Anonymous Zether but with simpler zero-knowledge proofs. PriDeFUL CT uses an updatable public key encryption scheme to achieve forward secrecy by introducing a new DDH-based construction in the standard model.In terms of transaction sizes, Quisquis (Asiacrypt, 2019), which is the only cryptocurrency that supports batchability (albeit in the UTXO model), has 15 times more group elements than PriDe CT. Meanwhile, for a ring of N receivers, Anonymous Zether requires 6 log N more terms even without accounting for the ability to batch in PriDe CT. Further, our implementation indicates that, for N = 32, even if there were 7 intended receivers, PriDe CT outperforms Anonymous Zether in proving time and gas consumption. © 2024 IEEE.
Author Keywords: Account-based Model; Blockchain; Forward Secrecy; Privacy-Preserving; Smart Contract; Zether
Publisher: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers Inc.

Authors: Dahibhate, R.; Patil, A.; Ganjewar, P.
Author full names: Dahibhate, Rohan (59227806700); Patil, Abhijeet (59325751000); Ganjewar, Pramod D. (56418545000)
Title: Smart Crowdfunding-Implementing Price-Triggered Transactions for Enhanced Decentralized Fundraising on Ethereum
Affiliations: School of Computer Engineering, MIT Academy of Engineering, Pune, MH, India
Authors with affiliations: Dahibhate, Rohan, School of Computer Engineering, MIT Academy of Engineering, Pune, MH, India; Patil, Abhijeet, School of Computer Engineering, MIT Academy of Engineering, Pune, MH, India; Ganjewar, Pramod D., School of Computer Engineering, MIT Academy of Engineering, Pune, MH, India
Abstract: Crowd-funding, also known as crowd financing or crowd-sourcing of funds, has emerged as a popular alternative method for raising capital in recent years. In the dynamic realm of cryptocurrencies, this paper introduces a groundbreaking transaction system tailored to give users the autonomy to establish and maintain personalized price conditions for Ethereum (ETH) transactions. In response to the inherent volatility of cryptocurrency markets, the core objective of this system is to ensure the legitimacy of funds conveyed to campaign fundraisers. Users are endowed with the authority to stipulate the quantity of ETH they intend to transmit, define the initial USD valuation of ETH at the transaction's inception, and set a predetermined timeframe within which the transaction is slated for execution. This comprehensive approach yields a transparent and secure mechanism, ensuring the precision of transaction processing even amidst the market's capricious fluctuations. In scenarios where market conditions deviate from the user's predefined price, the system adeptly redirects funds to the campaign raiser, integrating the value of ETH at the user-designated moment, thereby fostering heightened reliability in cryptocurrency transactions. © 2024 IEEE.
Author Keywords: Blockchain; Crowdfunding; Ethereum; Smart Contract
Publisher: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers Inc.

Authors: Spadea, F.; Seneviratne, O.
Author full names: Spadea, Fernando (58161571400); Seneviratne, Oshani (36697042800)
Title: Support and Scandals in GameFi dApps: A Network Analysis of The Sandbox Transactions
Affiliations: Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute, Troy, NY, United States
Authors with affiliations: Spadea, Fernando, Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute, Troy, NY, United States; Seneviratne, Oshani, Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute, Troy, NY, United States
Abstract: We explore the burgeoning field of GameFi through a detailed network analysis of The Sandbox, a prominent decentralized application (dApp) in this domain. Utilizing the bow-tie model, we map out transaction data within The Sandbox, providing a novel perspective on its operational dynamics. Our study delves into the varying impacts of external support, uncovering a surprising absence of enduring effects on network activity. We also investigate the network's response to several notable incidents, including the Ronin Hack and the United States Securities and Exchanges Commission's hearing on cryptocurrencies, revealing a generally resilient structure with limited long-term disturbances. A critical aspect of our analysis focuses on the 'whales,' or major stakeholders in The Sandbox, where we uncover their pivotal role in influencing network trends, noting a significant shift in their engagement over time. This research sheds light on the intricate workings of GameFi ecosystems and contributes to the broader discourse on the intersection of the Web, AI, and society, particularly in understanding the resilience and dynamics of emerging digital economies. We particularly note the parallels of the long-tail behavior we see in web-based ecosystems appearing in this niche domain of GameFi. Our findings hold significant implications for the future development of equitable and sustainable GameFi dApps, offering insights into stakeholder behavior and network resilience in the face of external challenges and opportunities. © 2024 IEEE.
Author Keywords: Blockchain; Bow-Tie Model; Data Analysis; Decentralized Application; Ethereum; Network Analysis; Public Perception; SAND; The Sandbox; Whales
Publisher: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers Inc.

Authors: Phatangare, S.; Tekade, O.; Thakare, P.; Wankhade, A.; Wankhade, P.
Author full names: Phatangare, Sheetal (58070638600); Tekade, Om (58502098800); Thakare, Prathamesh Prabhakar (58502060400); Wankhade, Abhishek Gajanan (58502170700); Wankhade, Purva (58502205100)
Title: CryptoDomains: Building a Decentralized Domain Marketplace with NFTs
Affiliations: Department of Computer Engineering, Vishwakarma Institute of Technology, Pune, MH, India
Authors with affiliations: Phatangare, Sheetal, Department of Computer Engineering, Vishwakarma Institute of Technology, Pune, MH, India; Tekade, Om, Department of Computer Engineering, Vishwakarma Institute of Technology, Pune, MH, India; Thakare, Prathamesh Prabhakar, Department of Computer Engineering, Vishwakarma Institute of Technology, Pune, MH, India; Wankhade, Abhishek Gajanan, Department of Computer Engineering, Vishwakarma Institute of Technology, Pune, MH, India; Wankhade, Purva, Department of Computer Engineering, Vishwakarma Institute of Technology, Pune, MH, India
Abstract: The proposed system introduces a decentralized platform built on the Ethereum blockchain, enabling users to acquire, trade, and utilize unique domain names as cryptographic identifiers. Leveraging the ERC-721 standard for non-fungible tokens (NFTs), the platform ensures the secure and transparent ownership of digital assets. The system integrates with the popular MetaMask wallet for user authentication, offering a seamless and widely-adopted solution. Users can mint new domains, buy existing ones, and list owned domains for sale, all facilitated by smart contracts governing domain creation, ownership transfer, and transaction execution. The architecture emphasizes security measures, such as code audits, access controls, and encryption, to safeguard user assets. The platform not only provides a novel approach to digital asset ownership but also simplifies cryptocurrency transactions by allowing users to conduct transactions using easily memorable domain names rather than complex wallet addresses. This research paper explores the technical intricacies, security considerations, and user interactions within the Crypto Domains ecosystem, contributing to the broader understanding of decentralized applications and non-fungible token platforms. © 2024 IEEE.
Author Keywords: Distributed ledger technology; Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC); MetaMask; Non-Fungible Tokens (NFTs); PoS (Proof of Stake); PoW (Proof of Work); Secure domain ownership; Secure Hash Algorithm (SHA-256); Smart contract

Authors: Doan, V.-T.; Trinh, T.-D.
Author full names: Doan, Thangthang (59217245600); Trinh, Tuan Dat (57213520515)
Title: Zero-Knowledge-Proof-Based Protocol Connecting Traditional Finance to Decentralized Finance
Source title: Lecture Notes in Networks and Systems
Affiliations: Hanoi University of Science and Technology, Hanoi, Viet Nam
Authors with affiliations: Doan, Thangthang, Hanoi University of Science and Technology, Hanoi, Viet Nam; Trinh, Tuan Dat, Hanoi University of Science and Technology, Hanoi, Viet Nam
Abstract: The rapid growth of the cryptocurrency market presents a compelling opportunity for traditional financial institutions to expand their customer base and services. To this end, they must collect the decentralized finance (DeFi) positions of their customers, but the customers are not willing to disclose their wallet addresses. To address this issue and connect the traditional financial market to the cryptocurrency market, this paper introduces an additional role for blockchain data analysis systems to avoid the direct connection between blockchain users and traditional financial institutions. There arise three challenges: (1) the user wallet addresses should not be disclosed to the institutions; (2) the institution must have confidence in the user DeFi positions provided by the blockchain data analysis system; and (3) the protocol must be flexible enough to be applied to a range of scenarios. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a tripartite protocol based on Zero-knowledge Proof and Merkle tree data structure. Leaf nodes in the Merkle tree store information on users’ DeFi positions. Zero-knowledge Proof helps users prove the accuracy and origin of their DeFi positions in the leaf nodes without revealing their wallet addresses. This paper discusses the strengths and limitations of the protocol, as well as the feasibility of applying the protocol in practice. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Singapore Pte Ltd. 2024.
Author Keywords: Blockchain; Cryptocurrency; Decentralized Finance; DeFi; Merkle tree; Security; Traditional Financial; Zero-knowledge Proof
Publisher: Springer Science and Business Media Deutschland GmbH

Authors: Takei, Y.; Shudo, K.
Author full names: Takei, Yuto (59320827300); Shudo, Kazuyuki (7102414517)
Title: FATF Travel Rule's Technical Challenges and Solution Taxonomy
Affiliations: Mercari, Inc., Tokyo, Japan; Kyoto University, Kyoto, Kyoto, Japan
Authors with affiliations: Takei, Yuto, Mercari, Inc., Tokyo, Japan; Shudo, Kazuyuki, Kyoto University, Kyoto, Kyoto, Japan
Abstract: Virtual assets are globally recognized as a decentralized digital currency system. They are also being used to transfer criminal proceeds. In 2019, the Financial Action Task Force mandated the Travel Rule for virtual asset service providers (VASPs). However, as of March 2024, it has not been fully implemented worldwide due to the Sunrise issue. Complying with the Travel Rule poses challenges such as identifying the recipient VASP from a virtual asset address, proving ownership of the address, and ensuring communication protocols between the VASPs. In this paper, we focus on these three challenges and provide potential approaches for each, along with additional considerations. We have analyzed multiple existing protocols and categorized their characteristics. Our findings revealed that the majority of them are based on an alliance of VASPs, while there are a few solutions that suggest peer-to-peer messaging for every VASP or blockchain as a communication hub. Additionally, we offer insights into open challenges that need to be solved in the long term. © 2024 IEEE.
Author Keywords: anti-money laundering; compliance; cryptocurrency; FATF; travel rule; VASP; virtual asset
Publisher: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers Inc.

Authors: Choi, S.; Xie, Z.; Tan, T.W.; Liu, Y.; Wu, Y.; Wang, X.
Title: Towards a Trusted and Cryptocurrency-Enabled Decentralized Wireless Community Network
Affiliations: Tsinghua University, Beijing, China
Authors with affiliations: Choi, Sungmin, Tsinghua University, Beijing, China; Xie, Zhuochen, Tsinghua University, Beijing, China; Tan, Tat Woo, Tsinghua University, Beijing, China; Liu, Yifan, Tsinghua University, Beijing, China; Wu, Yongqi, Tsinghua University, Beijing, China; Wang, Xingjun, Tsinghua University, Beijing, China
Abstract: In the context of the telecom network trending towards centralization, the relatively decentralized and citizen-centric, non-profit network architecture known as Wireless Community Network (WCN) has emerged. However, WCN faces challenges related to unintentional shifts towards centralization, the lack of automation and verifiability to handle increasing volumes of information, and the absence of real-time and more flexible incentive mechanisms to incentivize a diverse range of contributors based on the quality of their contributions. As the network expands, maintenance becomes more challenging for small volunteer teams, potentially compromising network performance, reliability, and overall trustworthiness. With the development of the decentralized physical infrastructure network (DePIN), this paper proposes a methodology for designing a decentralized wireless community network (DeWCN) system. This methodology includes the design of a consensus layer, a trust and reputation management layer, and presents incentive-driven oracle design methodologies for verifiable common resource pools. This is the first work in the DePIN domain discussing the design of DeWCNs. Unlike projects like Helium [1], we eliminate the need for specialized devices and mining-based token systems. © 2024 IEEE.
Author Keywords: Blockchain; Community Network; Decentralized Wireless Network; DePIN; Proof of Bandwidth
Publisher: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers Inc.

Title: 2024 IEEE International Conference on Blockchain and Cryptocurrency, ICBC 2024
Abstract: The proceedings contain 124 papers. The topics discussed include: incident analysis of decentralized finance; stability analysis of market-making mechanisms for decentralized cryptocurrency exchanges; unlocking DeFi literacy: understanding NFT market microstructure in the decentralized finance landscape; using machine learning for predicting arbitrage occurrences in cryptocurrency exchanges; protecting non fungible mutable tokens: an application in the metaverse; global, robust and comparable digital carbon assets; decentralized commit-reveal scheme to defend against front-running attacks on decentralized exchanges; towards the optimization of gas usage of solidity smart contracts with code mining; and a low-volatility strategy based on hedging a Quanto perpetual swap on BitMEX.
